The user's idea aligns with the "session-spanning memory" (from the design doc) but has a critical gap: the current accumulator only summarizes *current* turns, not what's being dropped. For this to work, the meta-agent would need to know which turns are leaving the window (i.e., the 10-turn window) and compact them.

But since the meta-agent is stateless, it can't track which turns are leaving unless it has external metadata (like turn numbers). The user's idea would require the system to know when a turn is about to be dropped (which the current design doesn't do).

Also, the user mentioned "if any turn is about to leave the window" - but the current system only has the last 10 turns (window size 10), so turns 11+ are automatically dropped. The meta-agent would need to know which turns are being dropped (but it doesn't - it only sees the last 10).
